researcher_notes: Delaware has a unique UCC search model among US states. It does NOT offer a public search portal. All UCC debtor searches (except 'search to reflect') must be conducted through a state-certified Authorized Searcher. The application process to become an Authorized Searcher is NOT publicly documented and appears to be a closed/invitation-only program for entities with established relationships with the state.

filing_office.agency_name: Delaware Division of Corporations (under the Delaware Department of State)
filing_office.description: The Division of Corporations within the Delaware Department of State handles all UCC filings and maintains the UCC index.

contact_information.agency_full_name: Delaware Department of State, Division of Corporations
contact_information.physical_address: John G. Townsend Building, 401 Federal Street, Suite 4, Dover, DE 19901
contact_information.mailing_address: Division of Corporations, PO Box 898, Dover, DE 19903
contact_information.office_hours: 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM ET, Monday-Friday (excluding state holidays)
contact_information.live_chat_hours: 8:30 AM to 4:00 PM ET, Monday-Friday (excluding state holidays)
contact_information.document_filing_service_hours: 7:45 AM to 11:59 PM Mon-Thu; 7:45 AM to 10:30 PM Fri (excluding state holidays)
contact_information.in_person_visits: By appointment only; call (302) 739-3077 to schedule

authorization_model.description: Delaware operates a formal 'Authorized Searcher' certification program. Effective December 1, 2001, ALL non 'Search to Reflect' UCC debtor searches must be performed by a Delaware Authorized Searcher. There is NO public search portal and NO way for the general public to directly search Delaware's UCC index. The state only provides certified searches -- there is no uncertified/plain search option. The Authorized Searcher program appears to be a closed program limited to Registered Agents, Service Companies, and Law Firms that have an established long-standing relationship with the state.

key_distinctions.authorized_searchers: Entities certified by the Division of Corporations to perform UCC debtor searches against the state's UCC Information System. There are currently approximately 25 authorized searchers.
key_distinctions.authorized_filers: Entities approved by the Division of Corporations to submit UCC filings electronically on behalf of customers. There are currently approximately 28 authorized filers. Since December 2015, all UCC filings must be electronic -- paper filings can only be submitted via Authorized Filers.
key_distinctions.search_to_reflect: A special post-filing verification search that confirms a UCC filing was properly indexed. This is the ONLY type of search not required to go through an Authorized Searcher.
key_distinctions.direct_e_ucc_filing: Any party can file UCC documents directly through the state's e-UCC web application or via XML submitters. Authorized Filers are only needed if submitting paper documents.

requirements_for_authorization.description: The Delaware Division of Corporations does NOT publish a formal application form, checklist, or public process for becoming an Authorized Searcher or Authorized Filer. The state website only states that authorized entities 'have had a long-standing relationship with the State of Delaware and have been Certified by the Delaware Division of Corporations.' This strongly suggests a closed/invitation-only program rather than an open application process.

known_eligibility_criteria.entity_types: Registered Agents, Service Companies, and Law Firms
known_eligibility_criteria.relationship_requirement: Long-standing relationship with the State of Delaware
known_eligibility_criteria.certification: Must be 'Certified by the Delaware Division of Corporations in performing online UCC filings and UCC searches'
known_eligibility_criteria.competency: Demonstrated competence in performing online UCC filings and searches

unknown_requirements.technology_requirements: Not publicly documented (likely access to the UCC Information System)
unknown_requirements.minimum_years_in_business: Not publicly documented but 'long-standing relationship' language is used

requirements_for_authorization.administrative_rules_document: https://corpfiles.delaware.gov/uccadminrules.pdf (PDF could not be parsed; may contain additional detail)
requirements_for_authorization.recommended_outreach: Contact the Division of Corporations UCC unit directly at DOSDOC_ucc@delaware.gov or (302) 739-3073 option 4 to inquire about the process and eligibility requirements for becoming an Authorized Searcher.
requirements_for_authorization.confidence: Medium -- The eligibility criteria are partially documented but the actual application/certification process is opaque and not publicly available

ucc_searching.primary_method: Through a Delaware Authorized Searcher (mandatory for all debtor searches except 'search to reflect')
ucc_searching.online_public_portal: None -- Delaware does NOT offer a free or paid public UCC search portal
ucc_searching.in_person: Not available for searches -- must use Authorized Searcher
ucc_searching.mail: Not available for searches -- must use Authorized Searcher
ucc_searching.search_logic: Exact name match (strict). Delaware adheres to exact name search logic with the exception of a few noise words. No name variations are included in search results. Searchers must manually input every name variation to generate a comprehensive certified listing.
ucc_searching.search_type: Certified only -- no uncertified/plain search option exists

ucc_filing.primary_method: Electronic filing via the state's e-UCC web application
ucc_filing.xml_submission: Available through various UCC XML submitters
ucc_filing.paper_filing: Only accepted through Authorized UCC Filers who submit electronically on behalf of the customer (since December 1, 2015)

search_fees.total_minimum_search_cost: $50.00 per debtor (search fee + mandatory expedite fee)
search_fees.ucc_11_expedited_fee: $25.00 (next day -- only expedited option for UCC-11)

copy_fees.certified_copies: $25.00 certification fee + $10.00 first page + $2.00 each additional page

filing_fees.ucc1_ucc3_via_authorized_filer: $100.00 for 1-4 pages; $100.00 + $2.00 per page exceeding 4 pages
filing_fees.additional_debtor_names: $25.00 per debtor name when more than two debtor names

fee_schedule.note: Authorized Searchers and Filers also charge their own service fees on top of state fees. These vary by vendor.

key_dates.2001-12-01: All non 'Search to Reflect' UCC searches must be performed by Authorized Searchers
key_dates.2015-09-08: Secured Party Assignment filings appear as 'Added' in search results; removals no longer separately indexed
key_dates.2015-12-01: All UCC filings must be submitted electronically; paper filings no longer accepted directly

items_requiring_outreach.item: Application process to become an Authorized Searcher
items_requiring_outreach.detail: No public application form or documented process exists. Must contact the Division directly.

items_requiring_outreach.item: Application process to become an Authorized Filer
items_requiring_outreach.detail: Similarly not publicly documented. Same outreach needed.

items_requiring_outreach.item: Whether new Authorized Searcher applications are being accepted
items_requiring_outreach.detail: The language about 'long-standing relationship' suggests this may be a closed program. Need to confirm if new entrants are accepted.
The PDF at corpfiles.delaware.gov/uccadminrules.pdf could not be parsed during research and may contain additional certification requirements or eligibility criteria.
items_requiring_outreach.contact_method: Request a copy from the Division or attempt direct PDF download

items_requiring_outreach.item: Technology/system access requirements for Authorized Searchers
items_requiring_outreach.detail: Authorized Searchers have 'special access to the state's internal lien index' but the technical requirements are not documented publicly.

items_requiring_outreach.item: Fees charged by Authorized Searchers (service company markup)
items_requiring_outreach.detail: Each Authorized Searcher charges their own service fees on top of state fees. These vary and are not regulated by the state.
items_requiring_outreach.contact_method: Contact individual Authorized Searchers for pricing
